title: The Frog and the Ox
lines: A Frog saw a large Ox and was struck with envy at his size.
"I am much smaller," he thought, "but I can make myself as big as he is."
The Frog began to puff himself up, swelling his body larger and larger.
"Am I as big as the Ox now?" he asked his companions.
The Frog continued to inflate himself more and more.
The Frog tried once more, puffing himself up to the utmost, when he burst and died.
moral: Do not overreach yourself; be content with what you are.
